info.name: iGOT Chatbot — Web/UI Proxy
info.description:
## iGOT Chatbot — Web/UI Proxy Collection

For frontend (web) integration. Auth is handled via browser session cookie — no separate JWT setup needed.

### How to get your cookie
1. Open https://portal.dev.karmayogibharat.net in Chrome
2. Log in to the portal
3. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies → find `connect.sid`
4. Copy the value and paste it into the `session_cookie` collection variable

### Typical flow
```
POST /sessions/create      → get session_id (auto-saved by test script)
GET  /sessions/list        → check if active session exists
GET  /sessions/history/{id} → load conversation thread (for resume)
POST /sessions/turn/{id}   → send user action, repeat until type:end
```

### Activity type → next action mapping
| Response activity type | What to render | Next action to send |
|---|---|---|
| `markdown` | Chat bubble with Markdown | Nothing — wait for next activity |
| `quick_replies` | Row of tappable buttons | `select_choice` with `choice_id` from `choices[].id` |
| `picker` | Searchable scrollable list | `pick_item` with `picker_id`, `item_id`, `item_label` |
| `input` | Free-text input box | `send_message` with `text` = what the user typed |
| `end` | End-of-conversation banner | Nothing — session is over |
| `text` | Plain text bubble | Only on session expiry — call POST /sessions/create to restart |

variable.description: Dev proxy base. Change host for UAT.
Auto-filled by the Start Session test script.
Browser session cookie. Get it from DevTools → Application → Cookies → connect.sid on the portal.

item.name: 1 — API Reference
item.description: All available endpoints, one each, in the order they are typically called. Use these as a reference or to test individual calls in isolation.

request.description:
Liveness probe for the proxy layer. No auth or cookie required.

Expected: `{"status": "ok"}`

If this returns a non-200, the proxy itself is down — no point calling any chatbot endpoints.

exec: // If an active session exists, save it so the next requests use it automatically
console.log('Active session found → session_id saved:', r.session_id);
console.log('No active session → run POST /sessions/create next');

header.description: Browser session cookie — the proxy reads this to identify the logged-in user. Get from DevTools → Application → Cookies → connect.sid.

request.description:
Call this first on every app open.

**Response — session found:**
```json
{ "session_id": "<uuid>", "status": "in_flow", "flow_id": "AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH" }
```
→ The test script auto-saves `session_id`. Call GET /sessions/history/{id} next to load the thread.
- `messages[]` always non-empty → render thread, last role:bot entry = current prompt
  (if user opened but hadn’t picked a topic yet, history returns the initial greeting + category menu)

**Response — no active session:**
```json
{ "session_id": null }
```
→ Call POST /sessions/create to start fresh.

Always returns 200 — never errors. Returns null when the session store is unavailable (safe fallback).

exec: // Save session_id — every subsequent turn request needs it
console.log('New session started → session_id saved:', r.session_id);

header.description: Browser session cookie — the proxy reads this to identify the logged-in user.

request.description:
Creates a new chat session and returns the opening greeting with a topic picker.

`channel`: `web` | `mobile` | `whatsapp` | `voice`
`language`: `en` | `hi` | any BCP-47 code

**Response contains:**
- `session_id` — saved automatically to `{{session_id}}` by the test script; used in every subsequent request
- `activities[]` — greeting message + topic picker (quick_reply buttons, one per supported topic)
- `status: "awaiting_user"`

Next: call POST /sessions/turn/{{session_id}} with the topic the user picks (send the `choice_id` from the quick_reply button they tapped).

header.description: Browser session cookie — the proxy reads this to identify the logged-in user.

request.description:
Returns every user action and bot response from the start of the session — so the UI can render a full scrollable chat thread, not just the last bot message.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from the Start Session test script.

**Response: `messages[]` array in chronological order.**
- `role: 'bot'` entries: render `activities[]` using the same rendering code as a /turn response
- `role: 'user'` entries: render `text` as a user chat bubble
- The last entry is always `role: 'bot'` — it is the current state awaiting user input

Always returns at least one bot entry — if the user opened the chat but had not yet picked a topic, returns the initial greeting + category menu (same as /sessions/create).

header.description: Browser session cookie — the proxy reads this to identify the logged-in user.

request.description:
Sends a user action and receives the bot's next activities. This is the core loop of the chatbot UI.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from the Start Session test script.

**Action types and when to use each:**

`select_choice` — user taps a quick-reply button:
```json
{ "action": "select_choice", "choice_id": "<id from choices[]>", "user_says": "<button label>" }
```

`send_message` — user types free text (only when `disable_input` is false in the current activity):
```json
{ "action": "send_message", "text": "user typed this" }
```

`pick_item` — user selects from a dynamic scrollable list (e.g. course picker):
```json
{ "action": "pick_item", "picker_id": "<from picker activity>", "item_id": "<id>", "item_label": "<label>" }
```

**Important:** `choice_id` and `picker_id` always come from the previous response's `activities[]` — never hardcode them in your application code.

Repeat until `activities[]` contains `{ "type": "end" }` — that means the conversation is complete and the session is closed.

item.name: Get session trace — NOT YET WIRED (501)

header.description: Browser session cookie — the proxy reads this to identify the logged-in user.

request.description:
Returns the full conversation trace (every node visited, branch decisions, API calls made). Useful for debugging flow logic.

Not yet implemented — returns HTTP 501.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from the Start Session test script.

item.name: 2 — Demo: AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH (complete flow)
item.description:
A verified end-to-end walkthrough of the AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH flow through the web proxy.

Run steps 1–5 in order. Step 1 auto-saves session_id; all other steps use {{session_id}}.

Full path: Start → pick topic AUTO_LOGOUT_CRASH → pick device Android → confirm resolved Yes → END

Only a browser session cookie is needed — no JWT or Kong token required.

exec: // Save session_id — used automatically in Steps 2–5 via {{session_id}}

header.description: Browser session cookie — set session_cookie in collection variables.

request.description:
Creates a new chat session.

Response: greeting message + topic picker (quick_reply buttons). The `session_id` is auto-saved to `{{session_id}}` by the test script — Steps 2–5 pick it up automatically.

item.name: Step 2 — Pick category: Application Crashing / Not Loading

request.description:
`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from Step 1.
`choice_id` — taken from Step 1 response, `activities[1].choices[].id`

Response: bot asks 'Are you on Android or iPhone?' with choices `android` and `ios`.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from Step 1.
`choice_id` — taken from Step 1 response, `activities[1].choices[].id`

Response: bot asks 'Are you on Android or iPhone?' with choices `android` and `ios`.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from Step 1.
`choice_id` — taken from Step 2 response, `activities[].choices[].id`

Alternative: send `"choice_id": "ios"` for iPhone-specific troubleshooting steps.

Response: numbered clear-cache / force-stop steps rendered as markdown, followed by 'Has the issue been resolved?' with choices `yes` and `no`.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from Step 1.
`choice_id` — taken from Step 3 response, `activities[].choices[].id`

Alternative: send `"choice_id": "no"` to escalate to a support ticket instead.

**Expected response:**
- `activities[0]`: `{ "type": "markdown", "text": "Glad I could help!" }` — render as chat bubble
- `activities[1]`: `{ "type": "end", "outcome": "self_served" }` — render end-of-conversation banner
- `status: "satisfied"`

After this, GET /sessions/list will return `{ "session_id": null }` — session is cleared.

`{{session_id}}` — auto-filled from Step 1.

Run this after Step 4 to see the full conversation thread that was just completed.

Expected: `messages[]` with bot entries (device question, clear-cache steps, resolved confirmation) and user entries (topic selection, Android, yes).

Note: the initial greeting and topic picker from Step 1 are not included — history starts from the first topic selection.

Optional verification step after the flow ends.

Expected: `{ "session_id": null }` — confirms the session was cleared after the `end` activity.

The test script asserts `session_id` is null and status is 200.
